title: Panic Attacks
lead: A panic attack feels like an emergency in your body — but it isn't dangerous, and it will peak and pass. Learning what's happening takes away much of its power.
explanation: A panic attack is a sudden surge of intense fear that triggers strong physical reactions: pounding heart, shortness of breath, dizziness, chest tightness, tingling, or a feeling of unreality. It can feel like a heart attack or like you're losing control — but a panic attack itself is not physically harmful.

What's happening is a false alarm. Your body floods with adrenaline as if facing danger, even though you're safe. The frightening sensations are that adrenaline at work. Because the feelings are so intense, we naturally fear them — and that fear fuels the next attack.

Panic attacks typically peak within about 10 minutes and then subside as the adrenaline burns off. You cannot stop breathing, faint from over-breathing, or 'go crazy' from a panic attack. Understanding this is often the first step to them losing their grip.
symptoms: Shortness of breath or choking feeling
Fear of dying or losing control

immediate.title: Slow the exhale
steps: Breathe in gently through your nose for a count of 4
Breathe out slowly through your mouth for a count of 6 or more
Make the out-breath longer than the in-breath

immediate.title: Ride the wave
immediate.description: Instead of fighting it, tell yourself: 'This is a panic attack. It always peaks and passes. I don't need to do anything but let it move through.' Resisting panic feeds it; allowing it lets it fade faster.

immediate.title: Ground into the present
steps: Press your feet firmly into the floor
Name out loud where you are and what today is
Remind yourself: 'I am safe right now'

longTerm.title: CBT for panic
longTerm.description: Highly effective. It helps you reinterpret the sensations as harmless and gradually stop avoiding places where panic has struck.

longTerm.title: Reduce the fear of fear
longTerm.description: Learning that the sensations can't hurt you — and even practising bringing them on safely in therapy (interoceptive exposure) — breaks the panic cycle.

longTerm.title: Steady breathing practice
longTerm.description: Practising slow breathing daily, when calm, makes it a reliable tool when panic hits.

resources.title: Understanding panic self-help guide
resources.whyHere: Free booklet explaining the panic cycle and how to break it.

resources.title: MindShift CBT
resources.whyHere: Free app with a dedicated panic-attack toolkit.

resources.title: How to cope with a panic attack
resources.whyHere: Practical, calm guidance from a leading anxiety non-profit.

matchPhrases: i think i'm having a heart attack
i feel like i'm losing control
i feel like i can't catch my breath
